notes: All 7 steps completed in one iteration. 12 claude bridge tests + 4 integration tests + 823 full suite tests pass with zero regressions. Wired claudeSendMessage/claudeAbort/claudeClearSession invoke wrappers, subscribeToClaudeEvents for all 7 event types, and App.tsx with real callbacks + event subscription + cleanup.

Post-review fix: added 3 rollback tests for subscribeToClaudeEvents partial failure (step-8), then replaced Promise.all with sequential try-catch-with-rollback pattern (step-9). All 826 tests pass across 53 files. All 9 steps complete.

summary: Add failing test for listener rollback on partial failure in gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts. Mock listen() to succeed for the first 3 calls (returning unlisten fns), then reject on the 4th call. Assert that: (1) subscribeToClaudeEvents rejects with the error from the 4th listen(), (2) all 3 previously-resolved unlisten functions were called (cleaning up orphaned listeners), (3) no listeners are leaked. Add a second test: listen() fails on the very first call — assert subscribeToClaudeEvents rejects and no unlisteners are called (none were registered). Add a third test: listen() fails on the last (7th) call — assert all 6 prior unlisteners are called before the rejection propagates.; Replace Promise.all in subscribeToClaudeEvents (bridge.ts lines 153-181) with a sequential try-catch-with-rollback pattern. Define the event-name/mapper entries as an array of tuples. Loop sequentially: const unlisteners: UnlistenFn[] = []; try { for (const [name, mapper] of entries) { unlisteners.push(await listen(name, mapper)); } } catch (err) { unlisteners.forEach(u => u()); throw err; } Return the combined unlisten function as before. This ensures that if any listen() call fails, all already-registered listeners are torn down before the error propagates to the caller.

Added 2 payload-mapping tests for claude-thinking-delta and claude-tool-result events. Both pass against existing spread-pattern implementation. Full suite: 828 tests pass, zero regressions. All 11 steps complete.
